title: Microsoft raises the price of Xbox Series X and S consoles worldwide
summary: The company increases prices by up to $150 due to the component crisis and discontinues the 2 TB model.

title: Apple increases prices of its devices due to rise in RAM memory
summary: The costs of NAND Flash and DRAM force Apple to raise prices on Mac, iPad and other products, with increases of up to $400.

title: Enshrouded leaves early access, GTA VI delayed and other news
summary: Enshrouded release date confirmed, a physical version of GTA VI is planned, and Valve talks about the Steam Deck 2.

title: IBM presents the first chip technology with transistors below 1 nanometer
summary: The Nanostack design stacks transistors vertically to continue miniaturization, reaching 7 angstroms.

title: ASUS restores TSME encryption on AM5 motherboards via a beta BIOS
summary: After the controversy, ASUS releases an update that restores memory encryption compatibility on X870, B850 and X670 boards.

title: AMD launches FSR 4.1, SDK 2.3 and an urgent hotfix for Adrenalin
summary: The new version of FSR offers improvements but reduces performance on RDNA 3, while installation issues on Windows 10 are fixed.

title: Valve corrects Steam Machine specifications and confirms a single RAM module
summary: The console will come with a 16 GB stick instead of two 8 GB sticks, and its target performance changes from 4K 60 FPS to 'up to 4K'.

title: Retailer LDLC mocks Steam Machine price with its 'Stim Machine' PC
summary: LDLC offers a system with Radeon RX 9060 XT for 999 euros, compared to the 1039 euros of Valve's console.

title: AMD re-launches the Ryzen 7 5800X3D and several processors drop in price
summary: Intel Core Ultra 9 285K and AMD Ryzen 5 9600X see significant drops, while Hygon introduces a 128-core CPU.

title: New peripherals: modular keyboard, gaming chair, retro controllers and secondary display
summary: Ergohaven, Razer, 8BitDo and Corsair present their latest accessories to enhance the computing and gaming experience.

title: Qualcomm presents its HBC architecture for compute-near memory
summary: HBC Gen 1 technology stacks LPDDR memory to achieve 133 TB/s bandwidth and six times more efficiency than HBM.

title: DIY alternatives to the Steam Machine emerge thanks to 3D printing and SteamOS
summary: SteamOS is now compatible with AMD Radeon graphics, allowing you to build a custom Steam Machine with 3D printing.

title: New SSDs and memory: deals, launches and market perspectives
summary: Swissbit and OWC launch new SSDs, ADATA presents its NFC drive, and Micron signs million-dollar agreements without foreseeing the end of the crisis.

title: Review of the MSI Claw 8, Sharkoon Fireglider One and HyperX Cloud Stinger 3
summary: The reviews highlight the performance of the handheld console, the ultralight mouse, and the low-cost wireless headphones.

title: Artificial intelligence sparks controversy: costs, security, and accusations of model theft
summary: From leaks about measuring AI effectiveness to reports of illegal distillation, the sector seeks answers.

title: AceMagic puts its AMD Ryzen Mini PCs on sale with discounts of up to 20%
summary: The promotion includes an exclusive coupon to purchase the systems at a reduced price.

title: Framework switches SSDs and announces price increase on its CPUs
summary: The company opts for ADATA XPG MARS 970 and warns of an increase in the cost of processors, while launching the Laptop 13 Pro motherboard.

title: Prime Day deals on hardware: GPUs, CPUs and motherboards with big discounts
summary: Tom's Hardware compiles the best deals of the event, while GIGABYTE promotes its motherboards and monitors.

title: Monitor deals and launches: Samsung 4K at $249 and Gigabyte QD-OLED 4K
summary: Samsung and B&H offer a 37-inch 4K monitor for $249, and Gigabyte launches its new QD-OLED gaming monitor.

title: Flash deals: GIGABYTE and Nintendo Switch 2 discounted
summary: GIGABYTE lowers prices on its motherboards and monitors, while the Switch 2 is available again at a reduced price on Woot.
